title: Chirality Sign
summary: The chirality sign is the plus or minus label attached to a Weyl node by the sign of the Jacobian determinant of the local linearized map.
assumptions: An orientation convention has been fixed.
scope: Lecture One sign data for isolated linear Weyl crossings.

source_anchors.notes: The determinant sign is the chirality label.

representation: Sign of the local Jacobian determinant.

symbols.meaning: chirality sign of the Weyl node

failure_modes: Sign conventions can differ between references.
retrieval_hints: Use when a query asks what plus or minus Weyl chirality means.
